todaysEvents.summary: Iran FM Araghchi declares Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' in direct contradiction of US blockade narrative; claims Iran has 'never closed' the strait; US Navy continues boarding Iran-bound vessels (Reuters, Al Jazeera, Apr 17).
todaysEvents.delta: De-escalation signal: Iran frames Hormuz as open — creates diplomatic space for deal

todaysEvents.summary: $20B frozen-assets deal under active negotiation: US proposes releasing Iranian assets held in South Korea and Japan in exchange for formal Hormuz sovereignty acknowledgment + nuclear commitment; second Islamabad talks confirmed for Sunday Apr 19 (NBC, CNN, Apr 17).
todaysEvents.delta: De-escalation: financial deal framework emerging; second talks round imminent

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $91.87 confirmed (TradingEconomics Apr 17, -2.9%); WTI ~$88.16 est. (-2.9%); de-escalation unwind drives energy and credit spreads lower; iTraxx Asia IG ~175bp (-7bp); Apr 22 ceasefire expiry remains binary catalyst.
todaysEvents.delta: Markets price meaningful probability of deal before Apr 22 ceasefire expiry

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Second round of US-Iran Islamabad talks (Apr 19) produces framework: $20B frozen-assets release + nuclear commitment + Hormuz sovereignty acknowledgment. Formal agreement signed before Apr 22 ceasefire expiry. P&I restoration phased over 2-3 weeks; Brent retraces toward $82-88/bbl as risk premium unwinds; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 155bp. Netanyahu-Lebanon track provides parallel de-escalation channel.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: US blockade and Iran-bound port closure persist; commercial Hormuz transit for non-Iranian flows continues with elevated insurance costs. Apr 19 Islamabad talks produce incremental progress but no framework before Apr 22 ceasefire expiry. Ceasefire extended short-term while negotiations continue. Brent stabilises $88-105/bbl; JKM $19-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 165-190bp. IRGC conducts asymmetric harassment without triggering full kinetic engagement.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Apr 19 Islamabad talks collapse; ceasefire lapses Apr 22 without extension. IRGC executes Gulf-wide shipping disruption — blocks Red Sea via Houthi reactivation, Gulf of Oman, and Persian Gulf. Full ceasefire collapse; resumed US-Israel air campaign. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco attack brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk becomes acute. Gold surges past $5,000/oz.
